title: First Record of the Root-Knot Nematode Meloidogyne africana on Albizia Trees in Karbala, Iraq‎
author: Abdalreda, Estabraq Mohammed
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: Albizia ( Albizia odoratissima ) trees in (Al-Husayniyah, University of Karbala, and Al-Hur,) Karbala province, Iraq were found with symptoms of yellowing, stunting, and galls in the roots, symptoms typical of infestation with root-knot nematodes. Morphological and molecular methods were used to identify the nematode species. Morphological characteristics and perineal patterns of the adults fit the description of  Meloidogyne africana . Moreover, the molecular analysis (PCR), of all screened samples with the specific primer 194/195 set also produced a 667-bp fragment, confirming that the root-knot nematodes isolated from albizia roots were  M .  africana . To the best of our knowledge, this is the first report of  M .  africana  infecting albizia trees in Iraq.

Moreover, the molecular analysis (PCR), of all screened samples with the specific primer 194/195 set also produced a 667-bp fragment, confirming that the root-knot nematodes isolated from albizia roots were  M .  africana . To the best of our knowledge, this is the first report of  M .  africana  infecting albizia trees in Iraq.
